SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 344 words

Tue May 5, 2026: NextEra Energy's Strategic Shift Towards Gas and Nuclear Fuels

NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is positioning itself as a key player in the evolving energy landscape, particularly in light of the 2026 'Reliability Shock.' On the 5th of May 2026, it was reported that NextEra Energy is set to finalize agreements within approximately three months for two Japan-backed natural gas-fired power projects in the United States, which are designed to support the burgeoning demand from data centers.

These upcoming projects, strategically located in Pennsylvania and Texas, are anticipated to generate nearly 10 gigawatts of power. This substantial capacity highlights the growing necessity for dependable energy sources, especially as AI-driven data centers increasingly require vast and reliable electricity supplies. While customer details for these projects remain undisclosed, the scale of investment underscores the swift transformation in power development driven by data center demand.

NextEra Energy's regulated utility, Florida Power & Light (FPL), is currently experiencing a surge in demand, with its pipeline of data-center requests expanding to 21 gigawatts. The company is in advanced discussions to fulfill more than half of that demand, with expectations for these projects to become operational by 2028. In addition to these initiatives, NextEra is also working to revive the Duane Arnold nuclear plant in Iowa, which is intended to provide power for Google data centers.

Moreover, the company is not solely focused on traditional power generation. NextEra Energy Resources has added 4 gigawatts of renewable and storage projects to its portfolio, bringing the total backlog to approximately 33 gigawatts. This diversified approach positions NextEra as a leader in the energy sector, capable of meeting the diverse needs of modern data centers while continuing to invest in sustainable energy solutions.

In its recent financial performance, NextEra Energy reported an adjusted earnings per share of $1.09 for the first quarter, surpassing analysts' expectations of $0.96. As the company continues to innovate and adapt to the demands of the energy market, it remains a focal point for investors looking at utility stocks. While there are opportunities in artificial intelligence stocks, the potential of NextEra Energy as a long-term investment cannot be overlooked.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 290 words

Sun May 3, 2026: NextEra Energy Charts Course in Utility Sector's AI-Driven Future

NextEra Energy is leading the transformation of utility stocks as the demand for electricity surges due to the expansion of artificial intelligence data centers. On 4th May 2026, the emergence of AI technology since 2022 has highlighted critical differences in how power providers manage grid strain and infrastructure readiness.

Investors are increasingly distinguishing between companies prepared for this surge and those lagging behind. NextEra Energy (NYSE: NEE) stands out as a primary example of a utility firm strategically positioned to thrive in this high-demand environment through modernization and expansion.

NextEra Energy, formerly recognized as Florida Power & Light, continues to provide electricity to 12 million residents in Florida. However, the company has evolved into one of the largest energy wholesalers in the United States, operating with 81 gigawatts of power production capacity. The company maintains ambitious growth plans, with the potential to approximately double its current output by the year 2032.

Beyond simple capacity, NextEra Energy is actively rethinking the operational framework of the electric utility sector. A major component of this strategy involves direct collaboration with major technology firms to address specific infrastructure needs. In December of last year, the company established a partnership with Alphabet's Google, focusing on designing and constructing data centers that are specifically engineered to be both power-efficient and self-sufficient from the ground up.

The company is also developing grid optimization solutions powered by AI to manage resources more effectively. NextEra Energy aims to establish 15 gigawatts of new production capacity specifically dedicated to data center hubs by 2035. These proactive measures coincide with projections suggesting that US data center power demand could grow 30-fold between 2024 and 2035, positioning NextEra Energy as a key player in negotiating favorable agreements in the evolving utility landscape.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 349 words

Sat May 2, 2026: Salt River Project Partners with NextEra Energy Resources for Major Solar Initiative

Salt River Project has announced a transformative power purchase agreement with NextEra Energy Resources, marking a significant step in the utility's renewable energy strategy. The deal, revealed on 4th May 2026, encompasses the development of 3,000 MW of solar generation alongside 1,000 MW of battery storage capacity, with construction slated to take place across Arizona and completion expected by 2027.

This initiative aligns with Salt River Project's broader objective to phase out coal-fired power generation by 2032. The solar and storage capacities combined will provide electricity for approximately 675,000 homes at peak output. Notably, the projects will utilize bifacial solar modules and tracking systems, which are designed to maximize energy capture in the challenging desert Southwest climate.

The 1,000 MW of battery storage is strategically aimed at addressing the evening peak period when solar production wanes while household energy demand rises. By storing solar energy for later use, Salt River Project intends to minimize its reliance on natural gas plants, thereby enhancing grid stability during periods of extreme heat. Furthermore, the utility anticipates that this significant buildout will not only generate tax revenue for local communities but also create numerous construction jobs over the next three years.

As of early 2026, Arizona has exceeded 11.4 GW of installed solar capacity, according to data from the Solar Energy Industries Association, with over 9,700 individuals employed in the state's solar sector. Solar power currently represents about 16% of Arizona's electricity mix, with expectations to add an additional 13.8 GW of capacity within the next five years. This expansion is indicative of the state's commitment to renewable energy and its potential for further growth in the sector.

In conjunction with this agreement, a comprehensive report on the solar cells and light-emitting diodes industry in the United States provides valuable insights into market dynamics, tracking demand, supply, and trade flows. The report benchmarks prices, margins, and trade routes, offering critical data for stakeholders in the solar landscape. This information is designed to aid manufacturers, distributors, and investors in navigating the evolving solar market and making informed decisions as they plan for the future.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

Nextera Energy, Inc., a Fortune 200 company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading clean energy provider in the United States, specializing in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity. As one of the world's largest producers of renewable energy, its core operations encompass a diverse portfolio that includes wind, solar, nuclear, and natural gas power plants, serving millions of customers across Florida and other regions. The company's fastest-growing segments are its renewable energy initiatives, particularly utility-scale solar and wind projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for sustainable power and supportive regulatory environments. This growth is driven by strategic investments in innovative technologies and infrastructure, positioning Nextera as a pioneer in the energy transition. With a strong commitment to operational efficiency and environmental stewardship, Nextera continues to deliver reliable energy solutions while advancing its goal of reducing carbon emissions. It is the NYSE's largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

Nextera Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is a clean energy utility with notable achievements in the renewable energy sector, spotlighted in recent NextEra Energy stock news. As an energy storage developer, NextEra significantly increases its energy generation capacity through strategic investments in NextEra wind power projects, solar power projects, and battery storage projects. Recent analyst upgrades, such as the NEE analyst upgrade $8, have led to the NEE price target increase, as Analyst ups NextEra Energy price target, prompting a positive response from NextEra Energy investor news. With its robust NextEra capital expenditure plan and commitment to carbon reduction initiatives, NextEra infrastructure projects and project pipeline reflect its long-term growth outlook. The company’s focus on utility-scale solar projects and offshore wind enhances its NextEra ESG performance. Analysts have responded to this promising trajectory, as evidenced by the NextEra Energy price target raised updates, highlighting their impact on renewable energy stocks NEE. Florida Power & Light (FPL) and NextEra Energy Resources are pivotal in expanding NextEra power purchase agreements and dividend yield.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

JUNO BEACH, Thursday, May 07 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Nextera Energy, Inc. has shown some weakness in recent days. Nextera Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE), the NYSE's largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, fell 2.2% during its previous trading session to close at $93.32.

NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is a leading energy company, recognized for its expansive renewable energy projects, including solar and wind farms that form the backbone of its diversified energy portfolio. Through subsidiaries like NextEra Energy Services, the company offers cutting-edge renewable solutions, enhancing its commitment to clean energy investments and ESG renewable strategies. Pioneering in AI-driven power solutions, its innovation pipeline focuses on grid modernization and smart grid solutions, underpinning its capacity expansion for AI demand. With utility-scale solar and energy storage for grid advances, NextEra Energy maintains a competitive edge in sustainable infrastructure projects and utility services.

SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 344 words

Tue May 5, 2026: NextEra Energy's Strategic Shift Towards Gas and Nuclear Fuels

NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is positioning itself as a key player in the evolving energy landscape, particularly in light of the 2026 'Reliability Shock.' On the 5th of May 2026, it was reported that NextEra Energy is set to finalize agreements within approximately three months for two Japan-backed natural gas-fired power projects in the United States, which are designed to support the burgeoning demand from data centers.

These upcoming projects, strategically located in Pennsylvania and Texas, are anticipated to generate nearly 10 gigawatts of power. This substantial capacity highlights the growing necessity for dependable energy sources, especially as AI-driven data centers increasingly require vast and reliable electricity supplies. While customer details for these projects remain undisclosed, the scale of investment underscores the swift transformation in power development driven by data center demand.

NextEra Energy's regulated utility, Florida Power & Light (FPL), is currently experiencing a surge in demand, with its pipeline of data-center requests expanding to 21 gigawatts. The company is in advanced discussions to fulfill more than half of that demand, with expectations for these projects to become operational by 2028. In addition to these initiatives, NextEra is also working to revive the Duane Arnold nuclear plant in Iowa, which is intended to provide power for Google data centers.

Moreover, the company is not solely focused on traditional power generation. NextEra Energy Resources has added 4 gigawatts of renewable and storage projects to its portfolio, bringing the total backlog to approximately 33 gigawatts. This diversified approach positions NextEra as a leader in the energy sector, capable of meeting the diverse needs of modern data centers while continuing to invest in sustainable energy solutions.

In its recent financial performance, NextEra Energy reported an adjusted earnings per share of $1.09 for the first quarter, surpassing analysts' expectations of $0.96. As the company continues to innovate and adapt to the demands of the energy market, it remains a focal point for investors looking at utility stocks. While there are opportunities in artificial intelligence stocks, the potential of NextEra Energy as a long-term investment cannot be overlooked.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 290 words

Sun May 3, 2026: NextEra Energy Charts Course in Utility Sector's AI-Driven Future

NextEra Energy is leading the transformation of utility stocks as the demand for electricity surges due to the expansion of artificial intelligence data centers. On 4th May 2026, the emergence of AI technology since 2022 has highlighted critical differences in how power providers manage grid strain and infrastructure readiness.

Investors are increasingly distinguishing between companies prepared for this surge and those lagging behind. NextEra Energy (NYSE: NEE) stands out as a primary example of a utility firm strategically positioned to thrive in this high-demand environment through modernization and expansion.

NextEra Energy, formerly recognized as Florida Power & Light, continues to provide electricity to 12 million residents in Florida. However, the company has evolved into one of the largest energy wholesalers in the United States, operating with 81 gigawatts of power production capacity. The company maintains ambitious growth plans, with the potential to approximately double its current output by the year 2032.

Beyond simple capacity, NextEra Energy is actively rethinking the operational framework of the electric utility sector. A major component of this strategy involves direct collaboration with major technology firms to address specific infrastructure needs. In December of last year, the company established a partnership with Alphabet's Google, focusing on designing and constructing data centers that are specifically engineered to be both power-efficient and self-sufficient from the ground up.

The company is also developing grid optimization solutions powered by AI to manage resources more effectively. NextEra Energy aims to establish 15 gigawatts of new production capacity specifically dedicated to data center hubs by 2035. These proactive measures coincide with projections suggesting that US data center power demand could grow 30-fold between 2024 and 2035, positioning NextEra Energy as a key player in negotiating favorable agreements in the evolving utility landscape.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 349 words

Sat May 2, 2026: Salt River Project Partners with NextEra Energy Resources for Major Solar Initiative

Salt River Project has announced a transformative power purchase agreement with NextEra Energy Resources, marking a significant step in the utility's renewable energy strategy. The deal, revealed on 4th May 2026, encompasses the development of 3,000 MW of solar generation alongside 1,000 MW of battery storage capacity, with construction slated to take place across Arizona and completion expected by 2027.

This initiative aligns with Salt River Project's broader objective to phase out coal-fired power generation by 2032. The solar and storage capacities combined will provide electricity for approximately 675,000 homes at peak output. Notably, the projects will utilize bifacial solar modules and tracking systems, which are designed to maximize energy capture in the challenging desert Southwest climate.

The 1,000 MW of battery storage is strategically aimed at addressing the evening peak period when solar production wanes while household energy demand rises. By storing solar energy for later use, Salt River Project intends to minimize its reliance on natural gas plants, thereby enhancing grid stability during periods of extreme heat. Furthermore, the utility anticipates that this significant buildout will not only generate tax revenue for local communities but also create numerous construction jobs over the next three years.

As of early 2026, Arizona has exceeded 11.4 GW of installed solar capacity, according to data from the Solar Energy Industries Association, with over 9,700 individuals employed in the state's solar sector. Solar power currently represents about 16% of Arizona's electricity mix, with expectations to add an additional 13.8 GW of capacity within the next five years. This expansion is indicative of the state's commitment to renewable energy and its potential for further growth in the sector.

In conjunction with this agreement, a comprehensive report on the solar cells and light-emitting diodes industry in the United States provides valuable insights into market dynamics, tracking demand, supply, and trade flows. The report benchmarks prices, margins, and trade routes, offering critical data for stakeholders in the solar landscape. This information is designed to aid manufacturers, distributors, and investors in navigating the evolving solar market and making informed decisions as they plan for the future.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

Nextera Energy, Inc., a Fortune 200 company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading clean energy provider in the United States, specializing in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity. As one of the world's largest producers of renewable energy, its core operations encompass a diverse portfolio that includes wind, solar, nuclear, and natural gas power plants, serving millions of customers across Florida and other regions. The company's fastest-growing segments are its renewable energy initiatives, particularly utility-scale solar and wind projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for sustainable power and supportive regulatory environments. This growth is driven by strategic investments in innovative technologies and infrastructure, positioning Nextera as a pioneer in the energy transition. With a strong commitment to operational efficiency and environmental stewardship, Nextera continues to deliver reliable energy solutions while advancing its goal of reducing carbon emissions. It is the NYSE's largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

Nextera Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is a clean energy utility with notable achievements in the renewable energy sector, spotlighted in recent NextEra Energy stock news. As an energy storage developer, NextEra significantly increases its energy generation capacity through strategic investments in NextEra wind power projects, solar power projects, and battery storage projects. Recent analyst upgrades, such as the NEE analyst upgrade $8, have led to the NEE price target increase, as Analyst ups NextEra Energy price target, prompting a positive response from NextEra Energy investor news. With its robust NextEra capital expenditure plan and commitment to carbon reduction initiatives, NextEra infrastructure projects and project pipeline reflect its long-term growth outlook. The company’s focus on utility-scale solar projects and offshore wind enhances its NextEra ESG performance. Analysts have responded to this promising trajectory, as evidenced by the NextEra Energy price target raised updates, highlighting their impact on renewable energy stocks NEE. Florida Power & Light (FPL) and NextEra Energy Resources are pivotal in expanding NextEra power purchase agreements and dividend yield.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

JUNO BEACH, Thursday, May 07 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Nextera Energy, Inc. has shown some weakness in recent days. Nextera Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE), the NYSE's largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, fell 2.2% during its previous trading session to close at $93.32.

NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is a leading energy company, recognized for its expansive renewable energy projects, including solar and wind farms that form the backbone of its diversified energy portfolio. Through subsidiaries like NextEra Energy Services, the company offers cutting-edge renewable solutions, enhancing its commitment to clean energy investments and ESG renewable strategies. Pioneering in AI-driven power solutions, its innovation pipeline focuses on grid modernization and smart grid solutions, underpinning its capacity expansion for AI demand. With utility-scale solar and energy storage for grid advances, NextEra Energy maintains a competitive edge in sustainable infrastructure projects and utility services.

SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 344 words

Tue May 5, 2026: NextEra Energy's Strategic Shift Towards Gas and Nuclear Fuels

NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is positioning itself as a key player in the evolving energy landscape, particularly in light of the 2026 'Reliability Shock.' On the 5th of May 2026, it was reported that NextEra Energy is set to finalize agreements within approximately three months for two Japan-backed natural gas-fired power projects in the United States, which are designed to support the burgeoning demand from data centers.

These upcoming projects, strategically located in Pennsylvania and Texas, are anticipated to generate nearly 10 gigawatts of power. This substantial capacity highlights the growing necessity for dependable energy sources, especially as AI-driven data centers increasingly require vast and reliable electricity supplies. While customer details for these projects remain undisclosed, the scale of investment underscores the swift transformation in power development driven by data center demand.

NextEra Energy's regulated utility, Florida Power & Light (FPL), is currently experiencing a surge in demand, with its pipeline of data-center requests expanding to 21 gigawatts. The company is in advanced discussions to fulfill more than half of that demand, with expectations for these projects to become operational by 2028. In addition to these initiatives, NextEra is also working to revive the Duane Arnold nuclear plant in Iowa, which is intended to provide power for Google data centers.

Moreover, the company is not solely focused on traditional power generation. NextEra Energy Resources has added 4 gigawatts of renewable and storage projects to its portfolio, bringing the total backlog to approximately 33 gigawatts. This diversified approach positions NextEra as a leader in the energy sector, capable of meeting the diverse needs of modern data centers while continuing to invest in sustainable energy solutions.

In its recent financial performance, NextEra Energy reported an adjusted earnings per share of $1.09 for the first quarter, surpassing analysts' expectations of $0.96. As the company continues to innovate and adapt to the demands of the energy market, it remains a focal point for investors looking at utility stocks. While there are opportunities in artificial intelligence stocks, the potential of NextEra Energy as a long-term investment cannot be overlooked.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 290 words

Sun May 3, 2026: NextEra Energy Charts Course in Utility Sector's AI-Driven Future

NextEra Energy is leading the transformation of utility stocks as the demand for electricity surges due to the expansion of artificial intelligence data centers. On 4th May 2026, the emergence of AI technology since 2022 has highlighted critical differences in how power providers manage grid strain and infrastructure readiness.

Investors are increasingly distinguishing between companies prepared for this surge and those lagging behind. NextEra Energy (NYSE: NEE) stands out as a primary example of a utility firm strategically positioned to thrive in this high-demand environment through modernization and expansion.

NextEra Energy, formerly recognized as Florida Power & Light, continues to provide electricity to 12 million residents in Florida. However, the company has evolved into one of the largest energy wholesalers in the United States, operating with 81 gigawatts of power production capacity. The company maintains ambitious growth plans, with the potential to approximately double its current output by the year 2032.

Beyond simple capacity, NextEra Energy is actively rethinking the operational framework of the electric utility sector. A major component of this strategy involves direct collaboration with major technology firms to address specific infrastructure needs. In December of last year, the company established a partnership with Alphabet's Google, focusing on designing and constructing data centers that are specifically engineered to be both power-efficient and self-sufficient from the ground up.

The company is also developing grid optimization solutions powered by AI to manage resources more effectively. NextEra Energy aims to establish 15 gigawatts of new production capacity specifically dedicated to data center hubs by 2035. These proactive measures coincide with projections suggesting that US data center power demand could grow 30-fold between 2024 and 2035, positioning NextEra Energy as a key player in negotiating favorable agreements in the evolving utility landscape.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 349 words

Sat May 2, 2026: Salt River Project Partners with NextEra Energy Resources for Major Solar Initiative

Salt River Project has announced a transformative power purchase agreement with NextEra Energy Resources, marking a significant step in the utility's renewable energy strategy. The deal, revealed on 4th May 2026, encompasses the development of 3,000 MW of solar generation alongside 1,000 MW of battery storage capacity, with construction slated to take place across Arizona and completion expected by 2027.

This initiative aligns with Salt River Project's broader objective to phase out coal-fired power generation by 2032. The solar and storage capacities combined will provide electricity for approximately 675,000 homes at peak output. Notably, the projects will utilize bifacial solar modules and tracking systems, which are designed to maximize energy capture in the challenging desert Southwest climate.

The 1,000 MW of battery storage is strategically aimed at addressing the evening peak period when solar production wanes while household energy demand rises. By storing solar energy for later use, Salt River Project intends to minimize its reliance on natural gas plants, thereby enhancing grid stability during periods of extreme heat. Furthermore, the utility anticipates that this significant buildout will not only generate tax revenue for local communities but also create numerous construction jobs over the next three years.

As of early 2026, Arizona has exceeded 11.4 GW of installed solar capacity, according to data from the Solar Energy Industries Association, with over 9,700 individuals employed in the state's solar sector. Solar power currently represents about 16% of Arizona's electricity mix, with expectations to add an additional 13.8 GW of capacity within the next five years. This expansion is indicative of the state's commitment to renewable energy and its potential for further growth in the sector.

In conjunction with this agreement, a comprehensive report on the solar cells and light-emitting diodes industry in the United States provides valuable insights into market dynamics, tracking demand, supply, and trade flows. The report benchmarks prices, margins, and trade routes, offering critical data for stakeholders in the solar landscape. This information is designed to aid manufacturers, distributors, and investors in navigating the evolving solar market and making informed decisions as they plan for the future.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

Nextera Energy, Inc., a Fortune 200 company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading clean energy provider in the United States, specializing in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity. As one of the world's largest producers of renewable energy, its core operations encompass a diverse portfolio that includes wind, solar, nuclear, and natural gas power plants, serving millions of customers across Florida and other regions. The company's fastest-growing segments are its renewable energy initiatives, particularly utility-scale solar and wind projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for sustainable power and supportive regulatory environments. This growth is driven by strategic investments in innovative technologies and infrastructure, positioning Nextera as a pioneer in the energy transition. With a strong commitment to operational efficiency and environmental stewardship, Nextera continues to deliver reliable energy solutions while advancing its goal of reducing carbon emissions. It is the NYSE's largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

Nextera Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is a clean energy utility with notable achievements in the renewable energy sector, spotlighted in recent NextEra Energy stock news. As an energy storage developer, NextEra significantly increases its energy generation capacity through strategic investments in NextEra wind power projects, solar power projects, and battery storage projects. Recent analyst upgrades, such as the NEE analyst upgrade $8, have led to the NEE price target increase, as Analyst ups NextEra Energy price target, prompting a positive response from NextEra Energy investor news. With its robust NextEra capital expenditure plan and commitment to carbon reduction initiatives, NextEra infrastructure projects and project pipeline reflect its long-term growth outlook. The company’s focus on utility-scale solar projects and offshore wind enhances its NextEra ESG performance. Analysts have responded to this promising trajectory, as evidenced by the NextEra Energy price target raised updates, highlighting their impact on renewable energy stocks NEE. Florida Power & Light (FPL) and NextEra Energy Resources are pivotal in expanding NextEra power purchase agreements and dividend yield.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

ALLENTOWN, Wednesday, May 06 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, PPL Corporation has shown some weakness in recent days. PPL Corporation (NYSE:PPL), the NYSE's 9th largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, dropped 1.3% during its previous trading session to close at $36.88.

PPL Corporation (NYSE:PPL) is a leading electric utility company primarily engaged in electricity distribution, power transmission, and regulated utility services. Serving residential, commercial, and industrial sectors, PPL Corporation is committed to grid modernization and the integration of smart grid technology and smart meters, enhancing storm response and reliability. As part of its strategic initiatives, PPL Corporation invests in infrastructure upgrades, distributed generation interconnection, and renewable energy integration, including battery energy storage systems to bolster energy efficiency programs. The company showcases its long-term competitive strengths through sustainability efforts and carbon reduction, fortified by renewable power purchase agreements and consistent dividend payments, making it an attractive utility stock.

SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 1, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 304 words

Wed Apr 8, 2026: Barclays Elevates PPL Price Target to $41, Maintains Overweight Rating

Barclays has raised its price target for PPL Corporation, increasing it from $40.00 to $41.00, reflecting a positive outlook for the utility company. On 10th April 2026, analyst Theresa Chen maintained an 'Overweight' rating for PPL, indicating a 2.50% increase in the target price, which suggests the firm's confidence in the company's performance in the regulated utilities sector.

PPL Corporation operates regulated utilities across Pennsylvania, Kentucky, and Rhode Island, providing essential services to millions. In Pennsylvania, the company is involved in the delivery and transmission of electricity, while in Kentucky, its subsidiaries LG&E and KU handle regulated electricity generation, transmission, and distribution, alongside natural gas distribution. Rhode Island Energy, another subsidiary, manages electric and gas utilities in Rhode Island, further diversifying PPL's utility portfolio.

According to data from 14 analysts, the average price target for PPL Corporation stands at $41.04, with estimates ranging from a low of $34.56 to a high of $45.00. This average suggests an upside potential of approximately 5.65% from PPL's current trading price of $38.85. The consensus recommendation from 17 brokerage firms indicates an average rating of 2.0 for PPL, categorizing it as 'Outperform' on a scale where 1 is 'Strong Buy' and 5 is 'Sell'.

Despite the optimistic outlook from Barclays and other analysts, estimates from alternative sources suggest that PPL Corporation could face a downside. The estimated fair value for PPL in one year is projected to be $36.42, indicating a potential decline of 6.24% from the current market price. These estimates are based on historical trading multiples and the company's past and future performance metrics.

The analysis and projections surrounding PPL Corporation reflect a complex landscape for investors, balancing bullish sentiment from major analysts with caution from alternative valuation assessments. Stakeholders are encouraged to monitor these developments closely as PPL continues to navigate the evolving utility sector.

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE

5.1 Activities

PPL Corporation is a major utility holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, headquartered in Allentown, Pennsylvania, and serving millions of customers across the United States. Its core business activities focus on the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity, as well as natural gas services, through regulated utilities in states like Kentucky, Pennsylvania, and Virginia. The company operates a diverse energy portfolio that includes thermal, hydroelectric, and nuclear power, emphasizing reliability and efficiency. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean energy and supportive regulatory environments. Additionally, PPL is investing in grid modernization and infrastructure upgrades to enhance resilience and support electric vehicle adoption, driving sustainable growth in a competitive energy market. This strategic focus positions PPL as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon economy. It is the NYSE's 9th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

PPL Corporation (NYSE:PPL) is a prominent electric utility focused on providing comprehensive electric distribution and power transmission services across its markets. As a leader in energy delivery, PPL Corporation offers both residential electric service and commercial electricity services, underpinning its role as a regulated utility service provider. It is committed to modernizing the grid through strategic initiatives such as grid modernization and smart grid advancements, which include implementing smart meters and enhancing outage management capabilities. PPL Corporation is actively pursuing renewable energy projects, integrating solar power and wind energy to bolster its clean energy transition. Through a robust capital investment plan, the company invests in utility infrastructure, with transmission system upgrades and substation maintenance enhancing reliability. PPL is committed to sustainable energy solutions, engaging in energy efficiency programs, carbon reduction initiatives, and advancing the electric vehicle charging infrastructure, ensuring its strategic positioning in a growing market.

urn:contentItem:6JHK-Y103-S8YC-S55J-00000-00ICYMI: Thursday's Preview: Can PPL Corporation (NYSE:PPL), rebound after being down?2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:09ZLexisNexisICYMI: Thursday's Preview: Can PPL Corporation (NYSE:PPL), rebound after being down?

NYSE STOCK REPORT

ALLENTOWN, Wednesday, May 06 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, PPL Corporation has shown some weakness in recent days. PPL Corporation (NYSE:PPL), the NYSE's 9th largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, dropped 1.3% during its previous trading session to close at $36.88.

PPL Corporation (NYSE:PPL) is a leading electric utility company primarily engaged in electricity distribution, power transmission, and regulated utility services. Serving residential, commercial, and industrial sectors, PPL Corporation is committed to grid modernization and the integration of smart grid technology and smart meters, enhancing storm response and reliability. As part of its strategic initiatives, PPL Corporation invests in infrastructure upgrades, distributed generation interconnection, and renewable energy integration, including battery energy storage systems to bolster energy efficiency programs. The company showcases its long-term competitive strengths through sustainability efforts and carbon reduction, fortified by renewable power purchase agreements and consistent dividend payments, making it an attractive utility stock.

SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 1, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 304 words

Wed Apr 8, 2026: Barclays Elevates PPL Price Target to $41, Maintains Overweight Rating

Barclays has raised its price target for PPL Corporation, increasing it from $40.00 to $41.00, reflecting a positive outlook for the utility company. On 10th April 2026, analyst Theresa Chen maintained an 'Overweight' rating for PPL, indicating a 2.50% increase in the target price, which suggests the firm's confidence in the company's performance in the regulated utilities sector.

PPL Corporation operates regulated utilities across Pennsylvania, Kentucky, and Rhode Island, providing essential services to millions. In Pennsylvania, the company is involved in the delivery and transmission of electricity, while in Kentucky, its subsidiaries LG&E and KU handle regulated electricity generation, transmission, and distribution, alongside natural gas distribution. Rhode Island Energy, another subsidiary, manages electric and gas utilities in Rhode Island, further diversifying PPL's utility portfolio.

According to data from 14 analysts, the average price target for PPL Corporation stands at $41.04, with estimates ranging from a low of $34.56 to a high of $45.00. This average suggests an upside potential of approximately 5.65% from PPL's current trading price of $38.85. The consensus recommendation from 17 brokerage firms indicates an average rating of 2.0 for PPL, categorizing it as 'Outperform' on a scale where 1 is 'Strong Buy' and 5 is 'Sell'.

Despite the optimistic outlook from Barclays and other analysts, estimates from alternative sources suggest that PPL Corporation could face a downside. The estimated fair value for PPL in one year is projected to be $36.42, indicating a potential decline of 6.24% from the current market price. These estimates are based on historical trading multiples and the company's past and future performance metrics.

The analysis and projections surrounding PPL Corporation reflect a complex landscape for investors, balancing bullish sentiment from major analysts with caution from alternative valuation assessments. Stakeholders are encouraged to monitor these developments closely as PPL continues to navigate the evolving utility sector.

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE

5.1 Activities

PPL Corporation is a major utility holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, headquartered in Allentown, Pennsylvania, and serving millions of customers across the United States. Its core business activities focus on the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity, as well as natural gas services, through regulated utilities in states like Kentucky, Pennsylvania, and Virginia. The company operates a diverse energy portfolio that includes thermal, hydroelectric, and nuclear power, emphasizing reliability and efficiency. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean energy and supportive regulatory environments. Additionally, PPL is investing in grid modernization and infrastructure upgrades to enhance resilience and support electric vehicle adoption, driving sustainable growth in a competitive energy market. This strategic focus positions PPL as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon economy. It is the NYSE's 9th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

PPL Corporation (NYSE:PPL) is a prominent electric utility focused on providing comprehensive electric distribution and power transmission services across its markets. As a leader in energy delivery, PPL Corporation offers both residential electric service and commercial electricity services, underpinning its role as a regulated utility service provider. It is committed to modernizing the grid through strategic initiatives such as grid modernization and smart grid advancements, which include implementing smart meters and enhancing outage management capabilities. PPL Corporation is actively pursuing renewable energy projects, integrating solar power and wind energy to bolster its clean energy transition. Through a robust capital investment plan, the company invests in utility infrastructure, with transmission system upgrades and substation maintenance enhancing reliability. PPL is committed to sustainable energy solutions, engaging in energy efficiency programs, carbon reduction initiatives, and advancing the electric vehicle charging infrastructure, ensuring its strategic positioning in a growing market.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJX-XY03-RT2N-146K-00000-00Scientists built a ‘zero-gap’ reactor that turns carbon dioxide into methane with 95% efficiency2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:09ZLexisNexisNEWSScientists built a ‘zero-gap’ reactor that turns carbon dioxide into methane with 95% efficiency

PC: Google Gemini Scientists at Pennsylvania State University came up with an amazing ‘zero-gap’ microbial electrosynthesis reactor. This device converts carbon dioxide into methane with more than 95 per cent efficiency. A crucial challenge in...

PC: Google Gemini Scientists at Pennsylvania State University came up with an amazing ‘zero-gap’ microbial electrosynthesis reactor. This device converts carbon dioxide into methane with more than 95 per cent efficiency. A crucial challenge in renewable en...

PC: Google Gemini Scientists at Pennsylvania State University came up with an amazing ‘zero-gap’ microbial electrosynthesis reactor. This device converts carbon dioxide into methane with more than 95 per cent efficiency. A crucial challenge in renewable energy storage gets tackled here by merging water electrolysis and biological methanation into one powerful unit. To make this work well, the reactor uses a membrane-separated electrode setup that cuts down electrical resistance inside, allowing fast electron transfer and steady reactions.The methane produced is renewable and can replace fossil fuels as a carbon-neutral option; it fits perfectly into existing natural gas systems for easy transport and storage. This development is a big step forward toward creating a circular carbon economy. It turns a major greenhouse gas into a high-energy fuel that's easy to store and can be scaled up for industrial use with precision.The ‘zero-gap’ design changes the structure by placing a thin membrane between the electrodes. This setup shortens the path that ions travel. Research headed by Bruce Logan at Penn State shows that this arrangement cuts down internal resistance and energy loss, resulting in a Coulombic efficiency of over 95 per cent, as reported by Pennsylvania State University. In other words, almost all the electrical energy is efficiently used for chemical conversion instead of being wasted as heat or lost in side reactions.Traditional methods often struggle with slow electron transfer to microbes; this reactor takes a different approach. First, renewable electricity splits water into hydrogen gas. Nearby, methanogenic microorganisms at the cathode immediately use this hydrogen to reduce carbon dioxide into methane. Because these organisms are so close, the process speeds up by avoiding delays that come from diffusion. As noted in a report by Bioengineer, the reactor produces almost 7 litres of methane for every litre of its volume each day. This high level of output is crucial for making it practical in industry. Since the result is methane, it works perfectly with the current global natural gas pipelines and storage systems. This offers a net-zero method for storing energy over long periods without needing to create new distribution networks.

May 6th, 2026 ( Transcript Daily - Delivered by Newstex )

Tesla, Coterra Energy, IREN, AltC Acquisition, Bloom Energy, GE Vernova, and onsemi are the seven Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Energy stocks are shares of companies involved in producing, transporting, refining, or supporting energyincluding oil and gas explorers and producers, pipelines, utilities, renewable energy developers, and equipment and service firms. For investors, these stocks tend to be sensitive to commodity prices, supply and demand dynamics, and regulation, and can offer either dividend income or growth potential depending on the company's business and exposure to the energy transition. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Energy stocks within the last several days.

Tesla (TSLA)

Tesla, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, leases, and sells electric vehicles, and energy generation and storage systems in the United States, China, and internationally. The company operates in two segments, Automotive, and Energy Generation and Storage. The Automotive segment offers electric vehicles, as well as sells automotive regulatory credits; and non-warranty after-sales vehicle, used vehicles, body shop and parts, supercharging, retail merchandise, and vehicle insurance services.

Read Our Latest Research Report on TSLA

Coterra Energy (CTRA)

Coterra Energy Inc., an independent oil and gas company, engages in the development, exploration, and production of oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids in the United States. The company's properties include the Marcellus Shale with approximately 186,000 net acres in the dry gas window of the play located in Susquehanna County, Pennsylvania; Permian Basin properties with approximately 296,000 net acres located in westTexas and southeast New Mexico; and Anadarko Basin properties with approximately 182,000 net acres located in Oklahoma.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CTRA

IREN (IREN)

IREN Limited, formerly known as Iris Energy Limited, owns and operates bitcoin mining data centers. The company was incorporated in 2018 and is headquartered in Sydney, Australia.

Read Our Latest Research Report on IREN

AltC Acquisition (ALCC)

AltC Acquisition Corp. does not have significant operations. It intends to effect a merger, capital stock exchange, asset acquisition, stock purchase, reorganization, or other business combination with one or more businesses. The company was formerly known as Churchill Capital Corp VIII and changed its name to AltC Acquisition Corp.

Read Our Latest Research Report on ALCC

Bloom Energy (BE)

Bloom Energy Corporation designs, manufactures, sells, and installs solid-oxide fuel cell systems for on-site power generation in the United States and internationally. The company offers Bloom Energy Server, a solid oxide technology that converts fuel, such as natural gas, biogas, hydrogen, or a blend of these fuels into electricity through an electrochemical process without combustion.

Read Our Latest Research Report on BE

GE Vernova (GEV)

GE Vernova LLC, an energy business company, generates electricity. It operates under three segments: Power, Wind, and Electrification. The Power segments generates and sells electricity through hydro, gas, nuclear, and steam power. Wind segment engages in the manufacturing and sale of wind turbine blades; and Electrification segment provides grid solutions, power conversion, solar, and storage solutions.

Read Our Latest Research Report on GEV

onsemi (ON)

onsemi is engaged in disruptive innovations and also a supplier of power and analog semiconductors. The firm offers vehicle electrification and safety, sustainable energy grids, industrial automation, and 5G and cloud infrastructure, with a focus on automotive and industrial end-markets. urn:contentItem:6JHR-TJ33-RTHD-V4WH-00000-00Top Energy Stocks To Keep An Eye On - May 7th2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:26ZLexisNexisTop Energy Stocks To Keep An Eye On - May 7th

TSLA: 3 Reasons the Stock Could Hit $400 in May

Tesla, Coterra Energy, IREN, AltC Acquisition, Bloom Energy, GE Vernova, and onsemi are the seven Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Energy stocks are shares of companies involved in producing, transporting, refining, or supporting energy-including oil and gas explorers and producers, pipelines, utilities, renewable energy developers, and equipment and service firms. For investors, these stocks tend to be sensitive to commodity prices, supply and demand dynamics, and regulation, and can offer either dividend income or growth potential depending on the company's business and exposure to the energy transition. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Energy stocks within the last several days.

Tesla TSLA

Tesla, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, leases, and sells electric vehicles, and energy generation and storage systems in the United States, China, and internationally. The company operates in two segments, Automotive, and Energy Generation and Storage. The Automotive segment offers electric vehicles, as well as sells automotive regulatory credits; and non-warranty after-sales vehicle, used vehicles, body shop and parts, supercharging, retail merchandise, and vehicle insurance services.

Tesla's Cybercab Is Finally Real-But Is It Enough?

Read Our Latest Research Report on TSLA

Coterra Energy CTRA

Coterra Energy Inc., an independent oil and gas company, engages in the development, exploration, and production of oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids in the United States. The company's properties include the Marcellus Shale with approximately 186,000 net acres in the dry gas window of the play located in Susquehanna County, Pennsylvania; Permian Basin properties with approximately 296,000 net acres located in westTexas and southeast New Mexico; and Anadarko Basin properties with approximately 182,000 net acres located in Oklahoma.

MarketBeat Week in Review - 04/20 - 04/24

Read Our Latest Research Report on CTRA

IREN IREN

IREN Limited, formerly known as Iris Energy Limited, owns and operates bitcoin mining data centers. The company was incorporated in 2018 and is headquartered in Sydney, Australia.

Read Our Latest Research Report on IREN

AltC Acquisition ALCC

AltC Acquisition Corp. does not have significant operations. It intends to effect a merger, capital stock exchange, asset acquisition, stock purchase, reorganization, or other business combination with one or more businesses. The company was formerly known as Churchill Capital Corp VIII and changed its name to AltC Acquisition Corp.

Read Our Latest Research Report on ALCC

Bloom Energy BE

Bloom Energy Corporation designs, manufactures, sells, and installs solid-oxide fuel cell systems for on-site power generation in the United States and internationally. The company offers Bloom Energy Server, a solid oxide technology that converts fuel, such as natural gas, biogas, hydrogen, or a blend of these fuels into electricity through an electrochemical process without combustion.

Read Our Latest Research Report on BE

GE Vernova GEV

GE Vernova LLC, an energy business company, generates electricity. It operates under three segments: Power, Wind, and Electrification. The Power segments generates and sells electricity through hydro, gas, nuclear, and steam power. Wind segment engages in the manufacturing and sale of wind turbine blades; and Electrification segment provides grid solutions, power conversion, solar, and storage solutions.

Read Our Latest Research Report on GEV

onsemi ON

onsemi is engaged in disruptive innovations and also a supplier of power and analog semiconductors. The firm offers vehicle electrification and safety, sustainable energy grids, industrial automation, and 5G and cloud infrastructure, with a focus on automotive and industrial end-markets. It operates through the following segments: Power Solutions Group, Advanced Solutions Group, and Intelligent Sensing Group.

Read Our Latest Research Report on ON

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

PPL Corporation is a major utility holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, headquartered in Allentown, Pennsylvania, and serving millions of customers across the United States. Its core business activities focus on the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity, as well as natural gas services, through regulated utilities in states like Kentucky, Pennsylvania, and Virginia. The company operates a diverse energy portfolio that includes thermal, hydroelectric, and nuclear power, emphasizing reliability and efficiency. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean energy and supportive regulatory environments. Additionally, PPL is investing in grid modernization and infrastructure upgrades to enhance resilience and support electric vehicle adoption, driving sustainable growth in a competitive energy market. This strategic focus positions PPL as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon economy. It is the NYSE's 9th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

Electric utility PPL Corporation (NYSE:PPL) is a leading provider in electricity distribution and power transmission, delivering regulated utility services with a focus on reliability. Offering residential electricity service and commercial electricity service alongside industrial power solutions, PPL Corporation is committed to enhancing grid modernization. Through the implementation of smart grid technology and the installation of smart meters, PPL ensures efficient outage restoration services, emphasizing storm response and reliability. Infrastructure investment is pivotal, including transmission upgrades and distributed generation interconnection, to accommodate renewable energy integration. With battery energy storage systems and demand response programs, PPL supports energy efficiency programs aimed at sustainability and carbon reduction. By engaging in renewable power purchase agreements and managing wholesale power supply, PPL addresses utility rate cases, optimizing customer billing and metering processes. As a dividend-paying utility stock, NYSE:PPL remains a key player, with PPL investor relations highlighting strong earnings guidance PPL and a price target by Barclays at $41, reflecting a positive Pembina business update.

SECTION 3 RECENT NEWS & PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

3.1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 304 words

Wed Apr 8, 2026: Barclays Elevates PPL Price Target to $41, Maintains Overweight Rating

Barclays has raised its price target for PPL Corporation, increasing it from $40.00 to $41.00, reflecting a positive outlook for the utility company. On 10th April 2026, analyst Theresa Chen maintained an 'Overweight' rating for PPL, indicating a 2.50% increase in the target price, which suggests the firm's confidence in the company's performance in the regulated utilities sector.

PPL Corporation operates regulated utilities across Pennsylvania, Kentucky, and Rhode Island, providing essential services to millions. In Pennsylvania, the company is involved in the delivery and transmission of electricity, while in Kentucky, its subsidiaries LG&E and KU handle regulated electricity generation, transmission, and distribution, alongside natural gas distribution. Rhode Island Energy, another subsidiary, manages electric and gas utilities in Rhode Island, further diversifying PPL's utility portfolio.

According to data from 14 analysts, the average price target for PPL Corporation stands at $41.04, with estimates ranging from a low of $34.56 to a high of $45.00. This average suggests an upside potential of approximately 5.65% from PPL's current trading price of $38.85. The consensus recommendation from 17 brokerage firms indicates an average rating of 2.0 for PPL, categorizing it as 'Outperform' on a scale where 1 is 'Strong Buy' and 5 is 'Sell'.

Despite the optimistic outlook from Barclays and other analysts, estimates from alternative sources suggest that PPL Corporation could face a downside. The estimated fair value for PPL in one year is projected to be $36.42, indicating a potential decline of 6.24% from the current market price. These estimates are based on historical trading multiples and the company's past and future performance metrics.

The analysis and projections surrounding PPL Corporation reflect a complex landscape for investors, balancing bullish sentiment from major analysts with caution from alternative valuation assessments. Stakeholders are encouraged to monitor these developments closely as PPL continues to navigate the evolving utility sector.

Recent News 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 365 words

Tue Apr 7, 2026: Pembina Pipeline Reiterates Commitment to Strategic Growth Outlook

CALGARY, Alberta–(BUSINESS WIRE)–Pembina Pipeline Corporation (“Pembina” or “the Company”) (TSX: PPL; NYSE: PBA) is set to hold a conference call and webcast to discuss its business update on 7th April 2026. The session will highlight Pembina's steadfast commitment to disciplined execution, detail its 3Cs Strategy—Capture, Connect, and Catalyze—and present a financial outlook that anticipates 5-7 percent compound annual fee-based adjusted EBITDA per share growth through the end of the decade.

Having established itself as a leader in North American energy infrastructure over more than 70 years, Pembina has earned the trust of its stakeholders by consistently delivering on commitments. The Company emphasizes its strong execution against financial targets, placing billions of dollars of capital projects into service on time while adhering to disciplined financial guidelines. Pembina's operational integrity is reflected in its ability to maintain an investment-grade credit rating and provide a reliable, growing dividend to its investors.

Central to Pembina's growth strategy is the 3Cs framework, which aligns with fundamental energy market trends. The Company is well-positioned to capitalize on the increasing global energy demand and the strategic importance of Canadian energy resources. The strategy encompasses three key priorities: Capture involves strengthening Pembina's core franchise through pipeline and gas processing expansions; Connect focuses on creating pathways for commodities to reach more lucrative markets; and Catalyze looks to develop new demand platforms in emerging sectors such as data centre power solutions and petrochemicals.

Looking ahead, Pembina is optimistic about its growth trajectory, targeting a 5-7 percent increase in fee-based adjusted EBITDA per share through 2030. This forecast is supported by anticipated higher utilization rates across existing assets and contributions from projects that have already been sanctioned. Pembina is also committed to investing in areas such as LNG, LPG, gas-to-power solutions, and emissions reduction infrastructure to meet evolving customer demands beyond the next decade.

With a strong asset base and a clear operational framework, Pembina Pipeline Corporation is dedicated to executing its strategy with precision. The Company aims to create long-term value for shareholders while adhering to its strong financial discipline. Stakeholders can access the live webcast of Pembina's conference call on the Company's website, where a presentation will also be available prior to the event.

3.2 PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

Story 1 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 88 words

PPL Accelerates Capital Program to $20 Billion Through 2028, Targeting 9.8% Annual Rate Base Growth

PPL Corporation is increasing its capital investment plan to $20 billion through 2028, a nearly 40 percent rise from previous guidance. This is expected to drive a 9.8 percent average annual growth in its rate base, reaching $38.6 billion by 2028. The investments will focus on grid resilience, transmission infrastructure, and generation modernization in Kentucky. A significant driver for this acceleration is the high demand from data centers. The program will be financed through a mix of equity and debt, supporting PPL's long-term earnings and dividend growth targets.

Story 2 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 75 words

PPL Reports Q3 2025 Earnings Beat at $0.48 EPS; Ongoing Earnings Up 14% Year-Over-Year

PPL Corporation announced Q3 2025 earnings of $0.48 per share, surpassing analyst estimates and marking a 14 percent increase year-over-year. Total operating revenue reached $2.24 billion, driven by returns on capital investments and favorable weather. Following the strong performance, PPL narrowed its full-year 2025 earnings forecast to $1.78-$1.84 per share, maintaining the midpoint. The company reaffirmed its long-term annual EPS growth target of 6-8 percent through 2028, citing disciplined cost management and constructive regulatory environments.

Story 3 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 86 words

PPL and Blackstone Infrastructure Establish Joint Venture for Natural Gas Generation Development in Pennsylvania

PPL Corporation and Blackstone Infrastructure Partners have formed a joint venture to develop natural gas-fired power plants in Pennsylvania to meet the rising electricity demand from data centers. PPL will hold a 51 percent stake in the venture, which aims to address a projected 6-gigawatt generation shortfall. The initiative, requiring an estimated $15 billion in capital, circumvents state regulations that restrict utilities from owning generation assets. The venture is structured to offer utility-like returns and is expected to contribute to PPL's earnings in the long term.

Story 4 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 83 words

Kentucky Public Service Commission Approves LG&E and KU Generation Expansion - $3 Billion CPCN Authorization

The Kentucky Public Service Commission has authorized PPL subsidiaries LG&E and KU to build two new natural gas power plants for $3 billion. The decision supports growing electricity demand, largely from data centers, and allows for the operational extension of a coal unit. While the commission approved the construction, it denied a special cost-recovery tariff, requiring the utility to seek cost recovery in future base rate cases. The approval is conditional on the projected load growth materializing, balancing economic development with ratepayer protection.

Story 5 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 89 words

PPL Electric Utilities Files Historic Base Rate Case - $356.3 Million Annual Increase Proposed

PPL Electric Utilities has filed its first base rate case in over ten years, seeking a $356.3 million annual revenue increase to fund grid modernization, improve reliability, and address aging infrastructure in Pennsylvania. If approved, the increase would raise a typical residential customer's monthly bill by about $12.39, or 7 percent. The Pennsylvania Public Utility Commission has suspended the request to initiate an investigation, with a final decision expected by July 2026. The filing follows an interim rate relief measure but is necessary for more comprehensive, long-term cost recovery.

Story 6 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 89 words

Rhode Island Energy Files Two-Year Rate Case - $230.1 Million Combined Increase Requested

PPL subsidiary Rhode Island Energy has requested a combined electric and gas rate increase of $230.1 million over two years, starting September 1, 2026. This is the first major rate filing since PPL acquired the utility and is intended to fund infrastructure modernization and accommodate load growth. The proposal, which targets a 10.75 percent return on equity, would increase a typical residential electric bill by about 5 percent in the first year. A decision from the Rhode Island Public Utilities Commission is anticipated in the third quarter of 2026.

Story 7 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 75 words

PPL Capital Funding Completes $1.15 Billion Exchangeable Senior Notes Offering

PPL's subsidiary, PPL Capital Funding, Inc., successfully completed a private offering of $1.15 billion in exchangeable senior notes due in 2030. The net proceeds of approximately $1.14 billion will be used to repay short-term debt and for general corporate purposes, enhancing the company's liquidity. The notes carry a 3.000 percent coupon and can be exchanged for PPL common stock at a premium, representing a favorable financing option that aligns with PPL's capital structure management strategy.

Story 8 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 70 words

PPL Electric Activates Dynamic Line Rating Technology - $23 Million Annual Congestion Cost Savings Realized

PPL Electric Utilities has deployed dynamic line rating (DLR) technology on three key transmission lines in Pennsylvania, making it the first U.S. utility to integrate DLR into real-time market operations. The technology dynamically adjusts transmission line capacity based on weather conditions, increasing efficiency and reducing congestion. PPL projects this initiative will generate $23 million in annual congestion cost savings and help avoid over $50 million in traditional infrastructure upgrade costs.

Story 9 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 73 words

Pennsylvania PUC Approves PPL DSIC Revenue Cap Increase - 5% to 7.5% Through 2027

In February 2025, the Pennsylvania Public Utility Commission approved PPL Electric Utilities' request to increase its Distribution System Improvement Charge (DSIC) cap from 5 percent to 7.5 percent of distribution revenues. This measure allows for quicker recovery of capital costs for infrastructure repairs and replacements between major rate cases. The approval was granted to address deteriorating system reliability and will provide interim funding for accelerated investments until a new base rate is implemented.

Story 10 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 75 words

Data Center Advanced-Stage Pipeline Surges to 20.5 Gigawatts in PPL Service Territories

PPL Corporation reported that advanced-stage interconnection requests from data centers across its Pennsylvania and Kentucky territories surged to 20.5 gigawatts in Q3 2025, a 42 percent increase from the previous quarter. This demand, driven by AI infrastructure, could double PPL's system peak demand in the next five to six years. While this creates significant revenue opportunities through new infrastructure investment, it also introduces execution risks related to the timing and realization of this projected demand.

urn:contentItem:6JH3-R5G3-S5C2-P1TH-00000-00Judge OKs $55M Deal In BP Archaea Suit2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:09ZLexisNexisNEWSJudge OKs $55M Deal In BP Archaea SuitJarek Rutz

The Delaware Chancery Court on Monday approved a $55.3 million settlement resolving derivative claims that Noble Environmental Inc.'s founders diverted a multibillion-dollar renewable energy opportunity to themselves through Archaea Energy, which BP later bought for $4.1 billion. Vice Chancellor Bonnie W. David granted the special litigation committee's motion over objections from the stockholder plaintiffs, finding that the...

The Delaware Chancery Court on Monday approved a $55.3 million settlement resolving derivative claims that Noble Environmental Inc.'s founders diverted a multibillion-dollar renewable energy opportunity to themselves through Archaea Energy, which BP later ...

The Delaware Chancery Court on Monday approved a $55.3 million settlement resolving derivative claims that Noble Environmental Inc.'s founders diverted a multibillion-dollar renewable energy opportunity to themselves through Archaea Energy, which BP later bought for $4.1 billion. Vice Chancellor Bonnie W. David granted the special litigation committee's motion over objections from the stockholder plaintiffs, finding that the two-member committee was independent, conducted a reasonable investigation and reached a reasonable compromise that will send cash directly to Noble's unaffiliated stockholders. "I have decided to approve the proposed settlement over the plaintiff's objections," Vice Chancellor David said during a hearing. The case stems from allegations by Noble stockholders Michael Schatzow and Scott Wedum that Noble's co-founders — Nicholas Stork, Richard Walton, Terrence Cunningham, and Alexander Sulkowski — used Noble's biogas technology, employees, and landfill assets to build Archaea Energy while leaving Noble's minority investors behind. According to the ruling, Noble was founded in 2016 as a Delaware environmental services company whose Westmoreland Sanitary Landfill in Pennsylvania produced biogas, a combustible gas derived from waste. In 2018, Stork and Walton began raising money for Archaea LLC, a new venture focused on biogas development, while materials indicated that Noble would have a significant stake in the new business. But Archaea's final formation documents gave Noble no equity stake, while Stork and Walton received incentive units and agreed to limit their work at Noble to its "existing business," the court said. Noble later received about $26 million when BP bought Archaea Inc. in December 2022, while Stork-affiliated entities received more than $200 million and Walton and an affiliated entity received about $143 million. After Noble's board formed the special litigation committee, or SLC, in June 2024, the committee retained Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati PC and spent roughly a year investigating the claims. Vice Chancellor David said the committee met 16 times, reviewed more than 150,000 documents from 25 custodians and conducted 10 witness interviews. The settlement requires a $55.3 million cash payment to Noble stockholders who are not settling parties. It also provides that Noble's Series B shares will be treated as owning 20% of the company's total outstanding equity. The settlement is tied to a proposed third-party acquisition of Noble for $755 million on a cash-free, debt-free basis, expected to result in roughly $350 million to $400 million being paid to Noble stockholders, subject to adjustments and escrow releases. The plaintiffs opposed approval, arguing the SLC was not sufficiently independent and did not adequately investigate or value the claims. They challenged committee member Andrew Aloe's relationship with a K&L Gates attorney who had represented Noble, questioned whether fellow committee member Matthew Newton was effectively Aloe's "mentee," and pointed to an August 2025 email from Stork disputing dilution issues as evidence that the committee was under pressure. Vice Chancellor David rejected those arguments, finding neither Aloe nor Newton had a disabling relationship with any defendant. She said K&L Gates and its attorney were not defendants, were not targets of the SLC's investigation and did not control the committee's work. She also said the record showed both SLC members were "present, involved and engaged" throughout the process. The plaintiffs also argued the SLC failed to fully address dilution claims, the validity of shares held by nonparty Sean Lehman, potential claims against additional Rice family defendants and other alleged Stork-related misconduct. The court found those issues did not undermine the committee's work, noting some were unpled or raised late, and that the SLC nevertheless considered them. On valuation, the court said the SLC relied on adviser Griffin Group, which estimated potential damages between $111 million and $968 million. Plaintiffs' own expert, Daniel Fishel, valued the claims at up to $2 billion, but the SLC found that analysis speculative and based on assumptions inconsistent with the evidence and business negotiations. Vice Chancellor David said the SLC reasonably considered the risks of continued litigation, advancement costs, uncertain damages and the benefit of an immediate cash recovery. She also said the proposed Noble sale supported approval, though it did not drive her analysis. "I am convinced that the proposed settlement is reasonable," Vice Chancellor David said. The court directed the parties to submit a revised order, including language requested by the plaintiffs and not opposed by the SLC, that the plaintiffs be included in the release of claims. SLC counsel Andrew Cordo of Wilson Sonsini also told the court the final order should clarify that it is a partial final judgment, because a fee application and confidentiality motions may remain pending. "I'm gratified that the Court approved the settlement and there is now a clear path for an acquisition that will provide tremendous returns to all shareholders," a spokesperson for Stork said. "I am extremely proud of Noble's achievements and look forward to continuing to build purpose-driven businesses that create lasting impact." Fox Rothschild LLP, representing Walton, declined to comment on the ruling. Other representatives for the parties did not immediately respond to requests for comment on Monday. Michael Schatzow is represented by Tibor L. Nagy Jr., Gregory N. Wolfe and Joseph Baier of Nagy Wolfe Appleton LLP and by Marcus E. Montejo, Caneel Radinson-Blasucci, Kirsten M. Valania and Brianna V. Manobianco of Prickett Jones & ElliotPA. Archaea Energy is represented by William E. Gamgort, Lakshmi A. Muthu and Kevin Rickert of Young Conaway Stargatt and Taylor LLP and by Richard C. Pepperman II and Christopher Michael Viapiano of Sullivan & Cromwell LLP. Nicholas Stork is represented by Vivek Tata of Elsberg Baker & Maruri PLLC and by Thomas W. Briggs of Morris Nichols Arsht & Tunnell LLP. Richard Walton is represented by Brett A. Berman, Sidney S. Liebesman, E. Chaney Hall and Griffin A. Schoenbaum of Fox Rothschild LLP. The Special Litigation Committee of the Board of Directors of nominal defendant Noble Environmental Inc. is represented by Katherine L. Henderson, Joseph Slights, Andrew D. Cordo and Andrew D. Berni of Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati PC. The case is Michael Schatzow v. Nicholas Stork, et al., case number 2024-0485, in the Delaware Chancery Court. --Editing by Vaqas Asghar.

May 9th, 2026 ( DailyPolitical - Delivered by Newstex )

Tesla, IREN, Bloom Energy, ExxonMobil, Coterra Energy, Chevron, and GE Vernova are the seven Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Energy stocks are shares of companies involved in producing, exploring, refining, transporting, or distributing energy resources such as oil, natural gas, and electricity. For stock market investors, the term usually refers to companies in sectors like oil and gas, utilities, and renewable energy that can offer returns tied to energy prices, demand, and regulatory changes. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Energy stocks within the last several days.

Tesla (TSLA)

Tesla, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, leases, and sells electric vehicles, and energy generation and storage systems in the United States, China, and internationally. The company operates in two segments, Automotive, and Energy Generation and Storage. The Automotive segment offers electric vehicles, as well as sells automotive regulatory credits; and non-warranty after-sales vehicle, used vehicles, body shop and parts, supercharging, retail merchandise, and vehicle insurance services.

Read Our Latest Research Report on TSLA

IREN (IREN)

IREN Limited, formerly known as Iris Energy Limited, owns and operates bitcoin mining data centers. The company was incorporated in 2018 and is headquartered in Sydney, Australia.

Read Our Latest Research Report on IREN

Bloom Energy (BE)

Bloom Energy Corporation designs, manufactures, sells, and installs solid-oxide fuel cell systems for on-site power generation in the United States and internationally. The company offers Bloom Energy Server, a solid oxide technology that converts fuel, such as natural gas, biogas, hydrogen, or a blend of these fuels into electricity through an electrochemical process without combustion.

Read Our Latest Research Report on BE

ExxonMobil (XOM)

ExxonMobil Corporation engages in the exploration and production of crude oil and natural gas in the United States and internationally. It operates through Upstream, Energy Products, Chemical Products, and Specialty Products segments. The Upstream segment explores for and produces crude oil and natural gas.

Read Our Latest Research Report on XOM

Coterra Energy (CTRA)

Coterra Energy Inc., an independent oil and gas company, engages in the development, exploration, and production of oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids in the United States. The company's properties include the Marcellus Shale with approximately 186,000 net acres in the dry gas window of the play located in Susquehanna County, Pennsylvania; Permian Basin properties with approximately 296,000 net acres located in westTexas and southeast New Mexico; and Anadarko Basin properties with approximately 182,000 net acres located in Oklahoma.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CTRA

Chevron (CVX)

Chevron Corporation, through its subsidiaries, engages in the integrated energy and chemicals operations in the United States and internationally. The company operates in two segments, Upstream and Downstream. The Upstream segment is involved in the exploration, development, production, and transportation of crude oil and natural gas; processing, liquefaction, transportation, and regasification of liquefied natural gas; transportation of crude oil through pipelines; transportation, storage, and marketing of natural gas; and carbon capture and storage, as well as a gas-to-liquids plant.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CVX

GE Vernova (GEV)

GE Vernova LLC, an energy business company, generates electricity. It operates under three segments: Power, Wind, and Electrification. The Power segments generates and sells electricity through hydro, gas, nuclear, and steam power. Wind segment engages in the manufacturing and sale of wind turbine blades; and Electrification segment provides grid solutions, power conversion, solar, and storage solutions.

Read Our Latest Research Report on GEV
